verses.text: David, fleeing his own son, cries that his foes multiply and many say God will not help him. Even the godly may be told their case is hopeless. The taunt that God has abandoned us is among the enemy's sharpest darts.

David, fleeing his own son, cries that his foes multiply and many say God will not help him. Even the godly may be told their case is hopeless. The taunt that God has abandoned us is among the enemy's sharpest darts.

Yet, says David, 'thou, O LORD, art a shield for me,' my glory and the lifter of my head; he cried and God heard him from His holy hill. Faith answers despair with God Himself. The one surrounded by enemies is still surrounded by his Shield.

Yet, says David, 'thou, O LORD, art a shield for me,' my glory and the lifter of my head; he cried and God heard him from His holy hill. Faith answers despair with God Himself. The one surrounded by enemies is still surrounded by his Shield.

He lay down and slept and woke again, for the LORD sustained him, so he will not fear ten thousands set against him. Peaceful sleep in danger is the fruit of trust. The soul resting in God can close its eyes amid a host of foes.

He lay down and slept and woke again, for the LORD sustained him, so he will not fear ten thousands set against him. Peaceful sleep in danger is the fruit of trust. The soul resting in God can close its eyes amid a host of foes.

'Arise, O LORD; save me,' he prays, confident that 'salvation belongeth unto the LORD,' whose blessing rests on His people. He looks to God alone for rescue. Deliverance is not in numbers or strength but in the God who saves.

'Arise, O LORD; save me,' he prays, confident that 'salvation belongeth unto the LORD,' whose blessing rests on His people. He looks to God alone for rescue. Deliverance is not in numbers or strength but in the God who saves.
